versions.content: Ranged is a keyword. An attack with the ranged keyword ignores the retaliate keyword. The character with retaliate does not deal damage back to the attacker.

A card that is in an upright state is ready. Ready cards can be exhausted to pay costs or trigger abilities. When a player readies a card, they rotate it from an exhausted (horizontal) state to a ready (upright) state.
Recover is a basic power. A player can exhaust their alter-ego to recover. When an alter-ego recovers, it heals damage equal to its REC (recovery) value.

Some abilities refer to other cards by title, trait, or other characteristics. A card that refers to its own title refers only to itself, unless the ability uses the phrase 'a [name] card'. References to traits work similarly to encompass all cards sharing that trait.

whats_new.diff: Complete revision of the interpretation of referential abilities. Clarification on the distinction between a card that names itself (self-referential) and a card that targets other copies with the same name.

versions.content: Resources are used to pay the cost to play cards and trigger abilities. The four types of resources are energy, mental, physical, and wild. A wild resource can be used as any of the other three types.
Retaliate X is a keyword. After a character with the retaliate X keyword is attacked, deal X damage to the attacker. The character with retaliate must survive the attack to deal this damage.

Revealing a card is the process of turning a facedown card faceup or revealing a card from the top of a deck. When an encounter card is revealed, its 'When Revealed' abilities trigger immediately. Encounter cards are revealed using a strict stepwise process.

whats_new.diff: Added a strict stepwise process for revealing encounter cards to properly resolve timing conflicts for interrupts and responses.
